_meta.title: AP Bio misconception taxonomy — CONSOLIDATION PROPOSAL (v2)
_meta.status: PROPOSAL — pending owner sign-off. NOT applied to the live taxonomy. Does NOT replace data/apbio_misconceptions.json.
_meta.purpose: Data-grounded consolidation of the 46-misconception v1 draft (data/apbio_misconceptions.json) down to 19 mid-grained misconceptions, driven by usage stats over the 240 drafted wrong-distractor tags in data/real_bio_eval_drafted.jsonl. Same two-layer contract: each surviving misconception still maps up to exactly one coarse cognitive-kind label in scripts/common_bio.py (content_gap | reasoning_error | misread_or_passage_mapping).
_meta.method: Merge by 'same attractive wrong belief / behaviorally indistinguishable from a single distractor'; drop over-fine zero-support tags that are not pedagogically central; RETAIN low/zero-support clusters that are pedagogically central (the harvested MMLU/SciQ/ARC eval is filler-heavy and under-exercises authored-only misconceptions, so absence in this eval != absence in the classroom).
_meta.usage_basis: data/real_bio_eval_drafted.jsonl — 80 items / 240 wrong-distractor draft tags (frontier_draft, needs_review).
_meta.target_number_justification: Requested band was ~12-20. The data alone (29 of 46 ids ever used; two tags absorb 25% of all tagged distractors; 50.8% overall no_fit) would support a smaller set (~13-15). We land at 19 — the top of the band — specifically because we chose to RETAIN pedagogically central clusters (cr_o2_role_confusion, enz_catalysis_model_error, map_passage_stem_mapping_error, ps_light_calvin_confusion) that this harvested eval barely exercises. Dropping them to hit a lower number would delete the exact misconception-bearing content the product must author-and-generate. So the number is justified as 'as small as the usage data wants, plus a small retained core the eval structurally cannot measure.'
_meta.coarse_validation_note: Every 'coarse' value below is one of common_bio.SUBSTANTIVE_LABELS, so the existing litmus load-time validator would still pass on this proposal.
honesty_flags: These tags are consolidations of DRAFT (frontier_draft, needs_review) distractor tags, not expert-reviewed gold. Usage counts describe the draft, not ground truth.

no_fit rate is 50.8% overall: half of harvested distractors carry no misconception at all (they are 'filler' — see docs/tagging_rubric.md). Consolidation cannot raise coverage of filler distractors; only authoring misconception-bearing distractors can.
Combined support = sum of the v1 usage of the old ids merged into each new id; it is a floor, since consolidation also captures distractors a rater couldn't previously split.

misconceptions.name: Botches the genetic-cross computation
misconceptions.description: Any error in working a Mendelian cross to a numeric/ratio answer: wrong Punnett fraction, genotype-vs-phenotype ratio swap, homozygous/heterozygous miscount, failure to assort two genes independently, or reporting a single-trait probability for a joint (both-traits) question. From one wrong numeric answer these are not reliably separable.

misconceptions.name: Wrong model of how alleles behave
misconceptions.description: A wrong mental model of allele inheritance: dominant allele must be more common, a masked recessive allele is destroyed/lost, or alleles blend into an intermediate rather than segregating as discrete units. From a single distractor (often an intermediate/absent phenotype) these read the same.

misconceptions.name: Ignores sex linkage
misconceptions.description: Treats an X-linked trait as autosomal, ignoring that inheritance patterns differ by sex.

misconceptions.name: Evolution is purposeful / goal-directed
misconceptions.description: The teleology family: evolution progresses toward perfection, traits evolve 'for the good of the species', organisms produce the mutations they 'need', or selection itself creates the needed variation. All treat evolution as a directed, purposeful force rather than differential reproductive success acting on pre-existing random variation. From one distractor these purpose-driven readings are behaviorally indistinguishable.

misconceptions.name: Lamarckian (soft) inheritance
misconceptions.description: An individual acquires adaptations during its lifetime in response to need, and traits strengthened/lost through use or disuse are passed to offspring. Inheritance of acquired characteristics.

misconceptions.name: Fitness means physical strength
misconceptions.description: Equates fitness with being strongest/fastest/'best' rather than relative reproductive success in a given environment.

misconceptions.name: Confuses oxygen's role/requirement in respiration
misconceptions.description: Any confusion about what O2 does and where it is needed: treats O2 as an electron donor (not the terminal acceptor), attributes ATP formation directly to O2, thinks the ETC runs anaerobically, or thinks glycolysis requires O2. RETAINED despite thin eval support because 'O2 is the terminal electron acceptor' is a central ETC concept.

misconceptions.name: Confuses what actually produces ATP
misconceptions.description: Misattributes the ATP-yielding step: believes fermentation itself makes ATP (rather than regenerating NAD+), treats NADH as a direct ATP product rather than an electron carrier, or reverses the pyruvate/lactate direction of fermentation.

misconceptions.name: Plants only photosynthesize
misconceptions.description: Believes plants photosynthesize instead of respiring, missing that they do both (respiration continues day and night).

misconceptions.name: Released O2 comes from CO2
misconceptions.description: Thinks the O2 released in photosynthesis is split from CO2 rather than from water (photolysis).

misconceptions.name: Confuses the light reactions and the Calvin cycle
misconceptions.description: Swaps inputs/products of the light reactions and the Calvin cycle, or assumes the Calvin cycle needs light directly rather than the ATP/NADPH the light reactions supply. RETAINED despite thin eval support because the light/dark-reaction split is core photosynthesis content.

misconceptions.name: Confuses osmosis direction / tonicity / what moves
misconceptions.description: A wrong model of osmosis: predicts water moving toward lower solute concentration, swaps hypertonic/hypotonic, expects solute (not water) to cross to balance concentrations, or reads a dialysis/osmosis mass change as solute movement rather than water movement. From one distractor these all read as 'wrong direction / wrong thing moves'.

misconceptions.name: Confuses active vs passive transport (energy)
misconceptions.description: Mislabels whether a transport process needs ATP: calls facilitated diffusion active, or thinks movement down a gradient (diffusion) requires cellular energy.

misconceptions.name: Mis-reasons about equilibrium and gradients
misconceptions.description: Assumes molecular movement ceases at equilibrium (rather than net movement being zero while molecules keep moving), or concludes 'no net movement' without reasoning that a concentration difference still exists.

misconceptions.name: Wrong model of what a catalyst does
misconceptions.description: A wrong model of enzyme catalysis: enzymes are consumed by the reaction, enzymes raise (rather than lower) activation energy, or enzymes shift the equilibrium / change delta-G rather than only speeding the approach to equilibrium. RETAINED despite zero eval support because 'enzymes lower activation energy and are reused' is a foundational enzyme fact.

misconceptions.name: Ignores enzyme (active-site) specificity
misconceptions.description: Ignores active-site specificity, treating an enzyme as able to catalyze unrelated reactions or act on any substrate.

misconceptions.name: Mis-predicts effect of conditions on enzymes
misconceptions.description: Mis-reasons about how physical conditions affect enzyme rate: assumes higher temperature always speeds the reaction (ignoring denaturation past the optimum), or assumes pH is irrelevant to activity.

misconceptions.name: Confuses variable roles or control
misconceptions.description: Mixes up independent vs dependent variable, or misidentifies the control group, when interpreting an experiment.

misconceptions.name: Mis-maps the passage/stem/figure to the question
misconceptions.description: The bio may be known but the passage-to-question mapping fails: overlooks a negation qualifier (EXCEPT/NOT/LEAST), draws a conclusion inconsistent with the figure/axis shown, or dismisses a described systematic trend as random error. RETAINED despite zero eval support because MCAT is passage-heavy and comprehension failure is a first-class error mode (mcat_pivot_spec.md 3.1).

crosswalk._note: old v1 id -> new v2 id, or 'dropped'. All 46 v1 ids are listed. 'content_gap (no misconception)' is NOT used as a mapping target here because none of the 46 v1 tags were a disguised content-gap bucket; the distractor-level content_gap verdict lives in the rubric, not the taxonomy.

dropped.enz_competitive_noncompetitive_confusion: Zero eval support and over-fine (competitive vs noncompetitive inhibition is a fine application distinction, not a mid-grained misconception). If it recurs, a rater should use enz_catalysis_model_error or flag taxonomy_gap (candidate).
